messages.content: reword: It is important to note that patients with hypertension should not be using decongestants due to their effects of increasing blood pressure.
It is crucial to remember that individuals with hypertension must refrain from using decongestants as they can elevate blood pressure.

The patient was advised to monitor their symptoms over the next 2 weeks. If the patient does not see improvement by then, they may have the option to switch their regimen or be referred to their doctor.

The patient was instructed to keep a close watch on their symptoms for the next 14 days. If there is no improvement in their condition by that time, they may have the alternative to change their treatment plan or to be referred to their physician.
The patient presented with a chief complaint of having trouble breathing through their nose and itchy watery eyes seasonal allergies
The individual came to the medical facility with the primary concern of experiencing difficulty inhaling through their nostrils and experiencing itchy, watery eyes, indicating seasonal allergies.
The patient reported that their symtpoms are generally the same everyday, occansionally worse in the morning
The patient communicated that they undergo similar symptoms consistently every day, with occasional exacerbation in the morning.
However, she has not started any treatment because she cannot remember which OTC medication she took in the past.
However, the patient has not initiated any treatment since they are unable to recollect which Over-The-Counter medication they had taken previously.
The patient says that taking hot steamy showers has helped with her nasal congestion
The patient mentioned that taking hot, steamy showers has aided in alleviating their nasal congestion.
The patient states that her symptoms become worse when she is outside for long periods of time.
The patient communicated that being outside for extended periods of time exacerbates their symptoms.
The patient should be advised to avoid their allergen/triggers if possible - for example, taking periodic breaks indoors when they are outside for long periods of time.
It would be recommended to advise the patient to try to avoid their allergen/triggers if feasible, such as taking intermittent breaks indoors when outside for prolonged periods of time.

Claratin is an antihistamine medication that is generally very well tolerated. This medication will not make you drowsy or interfere with your high blood pressure. 

You should see a relief in symptoms within 3-4 days however,it may take up to 2-4 weeks to see the full effects.

Claritin is an antihistamine drug that is mostly well-tolerated. This medication will not cause drowsiness or affect your high blood pressure. 

You can anticipate experiencing reprieve from your symptoms within 3-4 days. However, it might take up to 2-4 weeks to observe the complete benefits of the medication.
Choosing a pharmacotherapy method that best fits their needs.
The patient should be assisted in selecting a pharmacotherapy method that aligns with their needs.
